I have written many of them over the years for people, right? I've got hired by companies and I will tell you that I spend so much time on this first thing, this first thing, you know, this first few sentences. It's so crucial because it's like, you know, yeah, you need those first four seconds or you're perceived the wrong way, you're done. But as soon as those first four seconds are over, the conscious mind now kicks in and decides whether or not you were duped. In other words, if you don't continue to sound awesome in those first 15 seconds, if your conscious mind says, ah, yeah, nice try, you know, guys, guys, guys are moron, doesn't know what he's talking about. So in other words, you are still in real serious judgment mode in those first 10 seconds after the first four. So why leave that up to chance? It's like, honestly, to me, it baffles me how you could leave something so important up the chance.
